steps.title: Identify what the blank needs and how parentheses work
steps.content: The blank follows “three temporal” and needs a noun. A parenthetical that gives examples — “(summer of 1998, autumn of 2010, and winter of 2019)” — must sit directly after the noun it elaborates, with no intervening punctuation separating the noun from the opening parenthesis.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'spaces (summer of 1998, autumn of 2010, and winter of 2019)': “spaces” is followed immediately by “(” with no extra punctuation — the parenthetical sits cleanly after its noun. ✓
  • Choice B — 'spaces—summer of 1998, autumn of 2010, (and winter of 2019)': The em dash splits the list, then a parenthesis opens only around “and winter of 2019” — garbled punctuation that fragments the list. ✗
  • Choice C — 'spaces, (summer of 1998, autumn of 2010, and winter of 2019).': A comma before the opening parenthesis is not standard; commas do not introduce parentheticals, and the period inside the parenthesis prematurely ends the sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'spaces summer of 1998, autumn of 2010, and winter of 2019,': No punctuation or parentheses at all — the examples run directly into the text, making the sentence grammatically incoherent. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
